name: Easterling, Leslie
resolved_employer: Easterling & Miller Travel (owner/luxury travel advisor); independent affiliate of Brownell Travel and Virtuoso member
evidence: Leslie Easterling is a principal of Easterling & Miller Travel, an Austin-based luxury travel advisory that is an independent affiliate of Brownell Travel and a Virtuoso member; her Brownell advisor page and firm bio confirm a career from corporate event marketing/incentive travel into luxury family-travel advising. Both self-reported employer strings ('Easterling Miller Travel', 'Brownell Travel') and the Austin 78731 location match exactly.

name: Palmieri, Cosmo
resolved_employer: Service Group (Vice President; Service Group Holdings Inc.); owner of Sendero Homes, an Austin luxury/custom homebuilder
evidence: Cosmo Palmieri is Vice President at Service Group in Austin, TX and an owner of Sendero Homes (custom/luxury homebuilder, building in Austin since 1994); property records tie him to Austin-area real estate entities including SERVICE GROUP HOLDINGS INC, 4 POINTS PARTNERS LTD, SGP TEN ACRES LTD, HOLLOWS 1431 LLC and MADRONE CANYON LLC. Self-reported employers ('SGIFS Management', 'Service Group'), occupation 'Real estate', and Austin 78730 location all corroborate.

name: Marcus, Troy
resolved_employer: Evergen Equity, LLC (Founder) — Austin firm managing multigenerational private real estate for family offices across the southern U.S.
evidence: Troy Marcus founded Austin-based Evergen Equity in 2021 after a decade at Mimco, where he became president of Mimco's Central Texas (Austin) office in 2017; he has overseen more than $1B in acquisitions/developments/dispositions and Evergen holds ~2.5M sq ft across 24 properties in 11 cities. He also sits on the Advisory Board of Pennybacker Capital and chairs the YPO Real Estate Industry Network. Self-reported employer 'Evergen Equity', occupation 'Real Estate', and Austin 78701 location match.

affiliations.role: Advisory Board member (private equity real estate firm)

name: Mussey, Sarah
resolved_employer: None — most recently self-reported as 'Stay at Home Mom'; previously an analyst at American Campus Communities (Austin-based student-housing REIT)
evidence: Her own filings trace the arc: earlier gifts list 'Analyst / American Campus Communities' (the Austin-headquartered student-housing REIT, the largest U.S. developer/owner/manager of student housing), while her most recent gifts report 'Stay at Home Mom / none'. Identity beyond the self-reported employer could not be independently confirmed — the only 'Sarah Mussey' surfaced on LinkedIn is a Senior Design Manager at Social Llama Events, which does not match the reported analyst role, so that profile is likely a different person. Industry reflects her latest self-reported status rather than an outside source.

name: Heartfield, Melanie
evidence: Most likely Melanie McAllen Heartfield of Beaumont, TX — past president of the Junior League of Beaumont (2001-02) and wife of attorney J. Thad Heartfield of The Heartfield Law Firm (son of the late federal judge Thad Heartfield). The Beaumont 77706 zip and a Democratic-leaning federal giving history (20 FEC gifts, 0.79 lean) are consistent, but she reported no occupation or employer on any filing and no source establishes her own employment. Her Beaumont residence is also unexplained relative to a $1,545 total to Austin council member Ann Howard, so the identification itself is not firm. Industry deliberately left null.

name: Parker, James
evidence: Could not be identified. 'James Parker' is an extremely common name and this donor reported no occupation or employer on any of his filings from 2017 through 2026. The only corroborating details are an Austin 78757-2329 address and a small Democratic-leaning federal record (2 FEC gifts), which are not sufficient to distinguish him from the many same-named Austin residents. No industry assigned.

name: Haley, Brian
resolved_employer: CB Capital, LLC (co-founder and managing partner) — Austin real estate development and asset management firm
evidence: Brian Haley co-founded CB Capital, an Austin investment and real estate development firm, with Christian Alvarado; he holds a B.A. from UT Austin (where he was student body president) and a J.D. from UT Law ('09). Before CB Capital he was a vice president at private equity firm Limestone Capital Advisors developing and managing energy-related opportunities in the U.S. and Asia. He has led presidential national finance operations and raised more than $400MM for gubernatorial and senatorial candidates, which fits his heavily Republican federal giving record (67 FEC gifts, 0.016 lean). Self-reported employer 'CB Capital', occupations 'Investor/Managing Partner', and Austin 78703 location all match.

affiliations.role: Former Vice President — developed and managed energy-related investment opportunities in the U.S. and Asia (energy-sector private equity; firm's own bio does not specify oil & gas versus other energy)

affiliations.org: Opportunity Austin (Austin Chamber economic development initiative)

name: Hooper, Laurel
resolved_employer: Ware Private Jewelers, Austin (sales executive / jeweler)
evidence: Her self-reported employer across filings is Ware Private Jewelers (spelled 'Ware Private Jewelrs' on one filing) with occupations 'jeweler' and 'Sales'; Ware Private Jewelers is an Austin fine-jewelry firm run by GIA-educated professionals who previously owned Duncan & Boyd Jewelers of Austin. A LinkedIn profile for a Laurel Hooper listing the title 'Sales Executive' is consistent, but the profile is not publicly fetchable (LinkedIn blocks retrieval), so the match rests on the self-reported employer plus the Austin 78703 location rather than an independent source. One filing lists a different employer, 'UniversityMD', which could not be verified.

name: Renner, Kimberly
resolved_employer: The Renner Project (owner/proprietor) — Austin antiques dealership, design studio and showroom on North Lamar
evidence: Kimberly Renner owns and operates The Renner Project, an Austin store/workshop/warehouse specializing in mid-century modern, antique and vintage furnishings, which she and her husband Dan built out of a 16,000 sq ft former office space on the North Lamar corridor. She has restored historic Austin homes for more than twenty years and was dubbed the 'House Whisperer' by the Austin American-Statesman; she works as both a dealer and an interior designer. Self-reported employer 'The Renner Project', occupation 'Antique dealer/interior designer', and Austin 78705 location all match.

name: Wehbe, Bassam
evidence: Not confidently identified. A Bassam F Wehbe (age ~60) lives in Austin and is named as a son of the late Faez Najib Wehbe, whose obituary lists sons Bassam and Najib; Najib Wehbe is president of Wehbe Properties, an Austin real estate development firm at 5808 Balcones Dr — in the donor's own 78731 zip. That zip match is to a business address in a large residential zip and does not establish that Bassam works there, and he declined to disclose occupation or employer ('requested') on both filings. No source places Bassam Wehbe in any occupation, so no industry is assigned; the family real-estate connection is noted as a lead only, not a classification.

name: Gilliland, Thomas
resolved_employer: Fonda San Miguel (co-founder and sole owner) — Austin interior-Mexican restaurant
evidence: Tom Gilliland co-founded Fonda San Miguel with Miguel Ravago in 1975 — the first Texas restaurant devoted exclusively to authentic regional interior-Mexican cuisine — and is its sole owner; the restaurant marked its 50th anniversary with his book 'The Soul of Fonda San Miguel: Fifty Years of Food and Art' (UT Press). He first encountered Mexican cuisine in 1967 as a UT-Austin Law School exchange student in Mexico. Self-reported employer 'FondaSanMiguel', occupations 'Restaurateur/Owner', and Austin 78756 location (Fonda San Miguel is at 2330 W North Loop) match; a very heavy Democratic federal record (238 FEC gifts, 0.998 lean) fits his gifts to Mike Siegel and Marc Duchen.

name: Robinson, Brad
resolved_employer: Austin White Lime Company, Ltd. (managing director / director) — family-owned limestone quarrying and lime manufacturing firm near McNeil in Travis County
evidence: Brad Robinson, son of Spike Robinson, is one of the managing directors of Austin White Lime Company, Ltd., the 130-year-old Robinson family-owned limestone and lime producer near the Travis County community of McNeil; corporate records list him as a director. The firm makes quicklime, hydrated lime and lime slurry for soil stabilization, building construction, water treatment, steel and masonry. Self-reported employers 'AWL' and 'Austin White Lime' plus occupations 'Manager/President' and the Austin 78766 (PO box) location corroborate. Industry recorded as Construction because the taxonomy has no mining/building-materials label and the firm is classified under construction materials/contractors; note its products also serve oilfield applications, but that is a downstream product use and not an oil & gas affiliation. Federal giving is heavily Republican (46 FEC gifts, 0.011 lean).
